de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op

Class AbstractOperator

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op.AbstractOperator
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Named, Operator, SVSubstitute
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    AbstractSortedOperator, IfExThenElse, IfThenElse, SubstOp, UpdateApplication

    abstract class AbstractOperator
extends Object
implements Operator
    Abstract operator class offering some common functionality.

      private boolean isRigid 
      private Name name

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      protected abstract boolean additionalValidTopLevel(Term term)
      Allows subclasses to impose custom demands on what constitutes a valid term using the operator represented by the subclass.
      int arity()
      the arity of this operator
      boolean bindVarsAt(int n)
      Tells whether the operator binds variables at the n-th subterm.
      boolean isRigid()
      Tells whether the operator is rigid.
      Name name()
      returns the name of this element
      String toString() 
      boolean validTopLevel(Term term)
      Checks whether the top level structure of the given @link Term is syntactically valid, given the assumption that the top level operator of the term is the same as this Operator.

      • additionalValidTopLevel

        protected abstract boolean additionalValidTopLevel(Term term)
        Allows subclasses to impose custom demands on what constitutes a valid term using the operator represented by the subclass.

      • validTopLevel

        public boolean validTopLevel(Term term)
        Description copied from interface: Operator
        Checks whether the top level structure of the given @link Term is syntactically valid, given the assumption that the top level operator of the term is the same as this Operator. The assumption that the top level operator and the term are equal is NOT checked.
        Specified by:
        validTopLevel in interface Operator
        Returns:
        true iff the top level structure of the Term is valid.

      • toString

        public String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class Object
